description:
The reachability DFS inside compile_fn performs a linear scan of f.blocks on
every step:

  let blk = match f.blocks.iter().find(|b| b.id.0 == bid) {
      Some(b) => b,
      None => continue,
  };

This makes the DFS O(n_blocks²). For a function with 100 blocks (loops,
match chains) this is 10,000 comparisons instead of 100.

Fix: before the DFS, build a lookup map from the block list once:

  let block_by_id: HashMap<u32, &Block> =
      f.blocks.iter().map(|b| (b.id.0, b)).collect();

Then replace the find() with:

  let Some(blk) = block_by_id.get(&bid) else { continue; };

The map is O(n_blocks) to build and O(1) per lookup. Total DFS cost is
O(n_blocks + n_edges) instead of O(n_blocks * n_edges).

Proven: all existing fixture tests pass. The block_by_id map is local to the
reachability scope and can be dropped after it.

## LANDING THE WORK
  Land this ticket before starting the next one:
  - Commit only the changes for this ticket, referencing fz-cg1.8
  - Close the ticket (`bw close fz-v9l.8`); It will tell you if work is newly unblocked
title: fz-cg1.8 fix reachability DFS O(n2): pre-build block lookup map
